raw_responses:
{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "id": "GAP-001",
      "title": "Absence of SLAs and organ availability targets in MSP",
      "description": "The MSP Rules section is empty, providing no definitions for acceptable organ uptime, allowed degradation frequency, recovery time objectives, or overall system availability. The DIO currently operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation with no quantitative operational boundaries.",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "MSP",
        "section": "Rules",
        "detail": "The bootstrap seed contains no rules. The DIO operates under default-permissive MSP evaluation until Senate publishes substantive rules."
      },
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority_score": 27,
      "why": "Without SLAs, the DIO cannot measure or enforce organ health. The spine transitions (ALIVE↔DEGRADED) have no acceptable performance envelope, making it impossible to detect systemic degradation or trigger corrective action."
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-002",
      "title": "Undefined runtime targets and cadence baselines for organ health checks",
      "description": "The MSP specifies no runtime targets (e.g., ping intervals, missed-pong thresholds, recovery criteria). The spinal ping mechanism currently triggers DEGRADED on a single missed pong and ALIVE on a single recovered pong, with no hysteresis or multi-strike policy.",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "MSP",
        "section": "Rules",
        "detail": "No operational cadence or threshold parameters are defined anywhere in the MSP or referenced genome documents."
      },
      "criticality": "medium",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority_score": 18,
      "why": "The observed spine-state slice shows organs oscillating every 30–40 seconds. A single missed ping may be transient; without a defined threshold, the system may generate excessive state churn and mask real failures."
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-003",
      "title": "No organ prioritization or tiered availability requirements",
      "description": "All organs (e.g., Arbiter, Nomos, Graph, Axon) are treated identically in spine transitions, despite their differing criticality to the DIO's constitutional operations (BoR enforcement, action authorization). The MSP and its empty genome references do not differentiate organ criticality for availability, recovery, or escalation purposes.",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "MSP",
        "section": "Genome References",
        "detail": "Genome documents extend the MSP with organ-specific operating rules and risk-tier-specific constraints. None are defined at seed."
      },
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority_score": 18,
      "why": "Without tiered availability targets, a DEGRADED Arbiter or Nomos may receive the same operational response as a DEGRADED GitSync. This blurs the distinction between constitutionally critical organs and auxiliary ones, risking delayed response to high-impact failures."
    },
    {
      "id": "GAP-004",
      "title": "Missing operational governance in Bill of Rights",
      "description": "The BoR is entirely undefined (all articles empty). No scope of enterprise, prohibited activities, or core identity are defined. While the BoR primarily sets constitutional limits, its absence means there is no higher-level constraint on MSP rules, no fallback when MSP rules are insufficient, and no human-authoritative boundary for operational decisions.",
      "grounding": {
        "document": "BoR",
        "section": "All Articles",
        "detail": "All articles are marked '[To be defined by human principal]'. The BoR is a scaffold awaiting human authorship."
      },
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority_score": 27,
      "why": "The MSP is subordinate to the BoR. Without a BoR, the MSP has no constitutional grounding; any future MSP rules lack authoritative context. Operational parameters could drift without violating a defined scope, because none exists."
    }
  ]
}

{
  "gaps": [
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-001",
      "description": "No person-attention coverage for the human principal's engagement with MSP amendment approval notifications. The MSP Amendment Protocol mandates that an HOM notification is emitted to the human-principal mailbox and that a human decision (POST /amend/review) is required to activate any amendment. The current Minder contains no observations about Leon's responsiveness to governance notifications, his typical decision latency, or patterns of engagement with constitutional changes, leaving the system blind to the most critical relational handoff in the entire governance loop.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP -> Amendment Protocol (HOM notification and human principal approval step)",
      "criticality": "high",
      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "high",
      "priority": "high"
    },
    {
      "gap_id": "GAP-002",
      "description": "Total absence of person-attention coverage for the Bill of Rights authorship and amendment process. The BoR is a scaffold whose authority is explicitly reserved to the human principal only; no automated system may amend it. Every article—Scope of Enterprise, Prohibited Activities, Core Identity, Human Authority Reservation, Amendment Protocol, Interpretation Rules—remains undefined. The Minder contains zero observations about Leon's intentions, progress, availability, or communication preferences regarding this foundational document. Without this coverage, the DIO cannot track, remind, or contextually support the human's most sovereign relational obligation.",
      "grounded_in": "BoR -> Preamble (authority: human-principal-only) and Article V (Amendment Protocol, to be defined)",
      "criticality": "critical",
      "urgency": "high",
      "impact": "critical",
      "priority": "critical"

      "gap_id": "GAP-003",

The MSP Amendment Protocol specifies an HOM notification to the 'human-principal mailbox,' yet the Minder lacks any observation on whether Leon actively monitors that mailbox, what his preferred medium for urgent governance matters is, or his historical response times to similar notifications. This leaves the delivery mechanism for constitutionally required human decisions unverified and unadapted to the principal's actual attention patterns.",
      "grounded_in": "MSP -> Amendment Protocol (HOM notification requirement) and BoR -> Article IV (Human Authority Reservation, currently scaffold but structurally implied)",

      "urgency": "medium",
      "impact": "medium",
      "priority": "medium"

}
